files.latestApprovalRef: 2026-07-17 Dan chat approval ('approved as listed, plus posterTypes.ts'): editable poster text + subheadline revision fix — the poster headline/subheadline are now live-editable fields wired through both preview and publish (same builder inputs, so preview always equals publish); shared POSTER_TEXT_LIMITS (headline 28 / subline 32 / business name 34) are enforced visibly (input maxLength + counters, publish blocks with errPosterTextTooLong/errPosterTextNotAllowed) instead of silently clipping; sanitizePosterCopy records *_TEXT_ADJUSTED warnings whenever sanitizing changed requested text; subheadline/kicker/supporting-line feedback is recognized as copy-only client-side (no unwanted image regeneration or image-credit spend) and enforced server-side (requiresKickerChange intent, kicker included in visible-change fingerprint, kicker-unchanged candidates hard-fail, deterministic fact-safe kicker backstop REVISION_DETERMINISTIC_KICKER); oversized poster candidates are rejected at the gate (POSTER_HEADLINE_OVER_LIMIT / POSTER_KICKER_OVER_LIMIT) and the prompt states the hard poster fit limits and the subheadline->posterKicker revision mapping.
files.approvalRef: 2026-07-07 chat approval (final-launch-audit fix cycle): F-006 auto-bump a past one-time start time to now on draft resume and at publish; F-024 stop the empty AI poster kicker from falling back to a generic 'Try our' eyebrow (posterEyebrowLabel = null) so it never renders 'Try our any muffin'; 2026-07-08 Dan chat directive: sync the poster spec headline to the selected copy option, remove the per-option Why-this-angle rationale box; 2026-07-08 Dan chat directive: single-variant flow — remove the multi-variant copy picker; show one variant + always-visible refine panel; 2026-07-09 Dan chat approval (Approve both): honor merchant-edited headline/subheadline/CTA at publish; 2026-07-09 Dan chat approval (follow this plan): AI-generate cooldown live countdown; 2026-07-10 Dan chat approval ('you have permission to do all the others', ai-ads-publish-unblock-plan): Phase 3 honest errors — friendlyGenerationError now routes a photo/image failure with NO merchant photo attached to createAi.errImageServiceDown (keeps friendlyPhoto only when a photo was attached), and the publish mechanics-guard message createAi.offerMechanicsInvalid was reworded to point at the offer fields instead of blaming ad copy; Phase 4 diagnostics — pickPhotoFromLibrary catch now console.warns the underlying error; Phase 2.4 — a new eligibilityTouchedRef records offer-form fields the merchant edits by hand and is passed as touchedFields to mergeInferredEligibilityForm so free-text hint auto-inference never overwrites a manually edited field or flips a manually chosen offer rule; 2026-07-10 Dan chat approval ('I approve both'/'Phase 5 + Phase 6'): Phase 5 deterministic no-image publish — the two 'Every deal needs an image' guards (useFallbackTemplateAd and publishDeal) now allow a poster-style deal to proceed with no image (gated on showPosterFormat), since the consumer feed's ComposedAdCard renders the offer natively (deterministic_fallback, no baked-in text) and the deal row stores a null poster; usePhotoAsFinal is only set when a real photo exists, and the existing poster-spec->Standard-card fallback keeps publish fail-safe if the server rejects the imageless poster spec. Standard-card format still requires an image. No generation, prompt, or poster-layout logic changed; Phase 6 nav-reset fix — the custom-image-edit instruction TextInput onChange no longer calls resetGenerationState() per keystroke (which nulled generatedAd, collapsing the review UI to Step 1 mid-type and leaving the field holding one character); it now calls invalidateAcceptedAdDraft() so the image stays visible and the instruction is applied on the next Generate, matching the copy fields; 2026-07-11 Dan-requested double-check pass (same Phase 5 approval scope) — reachability completion: the Phase 5 gate-softening was inert because three upstream client gates still dead-ended the no-image poster flow. hasFallbackTemplateSource() now counts poster format as a fallback source, the generateAd NO_IMAGE_RETURNED branch derives its outcome from hasFallbackTemplateSource() instead of hardcoding ai_failed_no_fallback (so the recovery card shows Use fallback template), and acceptAd's no-image block is scoped to !showPosterFormat (acceptAd is the exact-preview approval path; the composite QA already passes deterministic_fallback and the publish presentation already sends the pre-existing 'deterministic-fallback' imageAssetId sentinel, so server validation accepts the imageless publish). No generation, prompt, or poster-layout logic changed
files.rationale: Controls AI ad creation, poster preview, revision, acceptance, and publish review behavior. Approved changes preserve authoritative deal facts, retry publish as a Standard card when hosted poster validation rejects only the poster spec, ensure the localized-owner-UI authoring locale follows the active business app language rather than stale profile language, and (2026-07-06) pass the owner's app language as contentLocale to the owner poster preview so it renders in the owner's language once the poster viewer-language flag is on. 2026-07-07: the Try another style button is hidden while the poster preview is shown (the poster template stays fixed, so cycling composed presentations changed nothing visible), and Android hardware back on a dirty draft now shows the same branded Discard changes dialog as the header back arrow instead of leaving silently. 2026-07-07 (F-006): for one-time deals only, a start time that has slipped into the past is clamped up to now — on draft load/resume (so the shown schedule is not stale) and again when building the publish payload (safety net if the draft sat open); recurring deals unchanged. 2026-07-07 (F-024): the empty-kicker eyebrow fallback constant posterEyebrowLabel is now null instead of 'Try our', so a poster whose AI kicker (copy.subline) is empty renders no eyebrow rather than the ungrammatical 'Try our <headline>'; the AdPosterCanvas 'copy.subline || eyebrowLabel' render logic is unchanged. No generation, template, copy, prompt, or poster-layout logic changed. 2026-07-08 (Dan directive): selectCopyOption now mirrors the chosen option headline into generatedAd.poster.copy.headline so the poster preview and the published spec follow the selection (deterministic offer lines and kicker untouched), and the per-option Why-this-angle rationale box was removed from the copy-option cards (strategy chip + CTA line stay; copyStrategyReasonKey retained per source-test contract). 2026-07-08 (Dan directive, single-variant flow): removed the multi-variant copy-options picker (the copyAlternativeOptions list, selectCopyOption/copyOptionMatchesAd/copyOptionsRepresentSameCandidate, copyStrategyLabelKey/copyStrategyReasonKey/compactReviewText helpers, the "N angles" count, per-option select, and the "Offer facts locked" badge). The screen now shows the single generated variant, and the "Ask AI for changes" refine panel is always visible (showComposedRevisePanel = !adAccepted) instead of being gated behind "Change words" (that button was removed). The merchant refines the one variant by comment. No generation, prompt, poster-layout, or publish logic changed. 2026-07-09 (honor merchant edits): draft-editor fields the merchant actually changed vs the accepted AI baseline (title/promoLine/ctaText, compared through aiDraftBaselineRef) now thread into generatedAdForPublishSpec as headline/short_description+subheadline/cta, so the published AdSpec, localization bundle, and owner-language preview carry the merchant's words instead of silently reverting to the AI draft; deals.title uses the merchant title via buildAuthoritativeDealDisplayCopy factSafeMerchantTitle only after checkMerchantDealTitleAgainstOffer passes, and a headline/subheadline that contradicts the locked offer facts blocks publish with the localized errHeadlineContradictsOffer message rather than being silently replaced. Unedited fields keep prior behavior exactly (AI copy + canonical deals.title); locked offer/terms lines remain fact-derived. 2026-07-09 (cooldown countdown): a COOLDOWN_ACTIVE 429 from ai-generate-ad-variants now drives a live per-second countdown on the disabled Generate button (generateCooldownCta label + cooldownCaption line, from an epoch-ms cooldownUntil deadline that self-corrects across app background/resume) instead of freezing a static 'Please wait Ns' string in the recovery card; the generate/revise catch paths route COOLDOWN_ACTIVE to beginGenerationCooldown (no error card/banner) and generateAd/reviseAd/canReviseAd are guarded while active, reverting automatically at zero. friendlyGenerationError's COOLDOWN_ACTIVE branch now returns the localized cooldownCaption rather than the raw English server message. No generation, prompt, poster-layout, or publish logic changed.

files.approvalRef: 2026-07-20 Dan chat approval (poster-ad quality harness, 'luminance-aware scrim'): legibility fix for text over photos. Root cause found + confirmed on device — the fresh template's dark-teal headline/business/offer-secondary/schedule (colors tuned for its light no-image gradient) were laid over photos, dropping the headline to ~1.1:1 contrast (invisible on dark subjects like coffee). Fix, applied to BOTH the V1 and V2 render paths: when an image is present, render those four text elements in light ink (POSTER_ON_IMAGE_HEADLINE #FFFFFF / POSTER_ON_IMAGE_MUTED #EFEAE1), keep the gold accent, and darken the TOP band with a luminance-aware scrim (topScrimAlphaForLuma sized from the optional spec.luma.top so already-dark tops get little/no scrim and bright tops get enough for >=3:1) — the bottom band already carries a heavy offer scrim, so it only needs the light ink. V2's fixed top scrim was removed in favor of the shared adaptive one. No-image posters are unchanged (topScrim 0, dark theme ink on the light gradient). Also added a dev-only forceLookV2 prop (undefined in production; only the __DEV__ poster gallery sets it for a V1-vs-V2 A/B). Deal facts, copy, templates, text limits, and generation are unchanged. Prior ref: 2026-07-06 chat approval: poster quality v2 handoff (POSTER_QUALITY_V2_SONNET_HANDOFF.md); 2026-07-08 Dan chat directive (F-024 deterministic): blank generic AI kickers (Try our / Our deal / Special offer / Menu pick) at render
files.rationale: Renders the approved native poster layout, image treatment, and text overlay placement. 2026-07-06: added an optional contentLocale prop and a flag-gated locale-aware copy resolution path (posterCopyFromSpecForLocale, using the existing posterCopyForLocale helper) behind EXPO_PUBLIC_POSTER_VIEWER_LANGUAGE_ENABLED; also added a sibling Poster Look v2 render path (PosterContentV2/TopCopyBlockV2/OfferBlockV2) behind EXPO_PUBLIC_POSTER_LOOK_V2 with Outfit_900Black/BlackHanSans_400Regular display typography, an offer-badge chip, and lighter top/bottom scrims. Both flags default off; the original PosterContent/TopCopyBlock/OfferBlock v1 components and constants are unmodified, so behavior is byte-identical with both flags off. 2026-07-08 (F-024 deterministic enforcement): sanitizedPosterEyebrow blanks exactly the generic kickers the generation prompt already forbids (Try our, Our deal, Special offer, Menu pick) on both the v1 and v2 top blocks, covering owner previews and already-stored consumer specs; all other kickers render unchanged.

files.latestApprovalRef: 2026-07-18 Dan chat approval ('complete the proposed fix'): end the item-name validation catch-22 that returned COPY_FAILED 502 for item names embedding parenthetical descriptions with banned words (prod repro: 'the recon roast ( Roaster fresh coffee with a shot of espresso)') — (1) banned hype-word checks (UNSUPPORTED_PROMO_CLAIM) now run on copy text with the contract's exact item names masked out, so words inside a merchant item name can never invalidate copy while hype words elsewhere still fail; (2) containsItem also accepts the core item name with parenthetical segments stripped, so copy naming the core item passes the item checks without carrying the whole description; (3) buildDeterministicDealChannelCopy builds channel copy from paren-stripped core names so the deterministic fallback fits field limits and always validates instead of throwing DETERMINISTIC_FALLBACK_INVALID. The locked canonicalOfferLine and offer facts are unchanged. Prior ref: 2026-07-14 Dan chat approval: strip stray wrapping/unmatched quote characters from offer item names before contract canonicalization, validation, and deterministic fallback copy so inferred items like "THE SERGEANT'S STRIPES (...) do not cause COPY_FAILED. No prompt, provider, schema, poster layout, migration, or production deploy performed.
files.approvalRef: 2026-07-03 initial AI poster core lock; 2026-07-13 Dan chat approval: keep the punctuation-only location-name cleanup in canonical offer terms
files.rationale: Keeps deal facts authoritative for AI generation and poster offer lines. 2026-07-13: canonical free-item and percent-off terms strip trailing punctuation from the location name before adding the sentence period, preventing duplicated punctuation such as 'Co..' without changing offer facts, prompt behavior, poster layout, or deployment requirements. 2026-07-20: validateBuyOneGetOneFree no longer assumes the reward item equals the purchased item. buildDealOfferContract keeps a distinct freeItemDescription on a BUY_ONE_GET_ONE_FREE contract, so for a 'buy a drip coffee, get a free cookie' offer the module generated canonicalOfferLine 'Buy a drip coffee and get a free cookie' and then rejected that same self-generated line with CHANGES_FREE_ITEM, blocking publish with no free-item field in the BOGO form for the merchant to correct. Free-item mentions are now checked against freeReward.itemName (falling back to the purchased item) while MISSING_BOGO_ITEM still checks the purchased item. Offer facts, canonical offer lines, prompts, poster layout, and deploy requirements are unchanged; this only stops a false rejection. 2026-07-20 (second change, same approval): buildDealOfferContract no longer derives the reward item from freeItemDescription for BUY_ONE_GET_ONE_FREE. That deal type is same-item by definition and its create form exposes no free-item input, so the field could only ever carry stale state — on device a 'drip coffee' BOGO shipped a contract with free_reward 'Bu' (a free-text parser fragment), which blocked publish with CHANGES_FREE_ITEM and made the ai-generate-ad-variants edge function fail with COPY_FAILED because the AI was asked to write copy for an item that does not exist. The reward is now always the purchased item for BOGO; BUY_ONE_GET_SOMETHING_FREE still reads freeItemDescription so genuinely different rewards are unaffected. This corrects contaminated offer facts rather than changing intended ones; prompts, poster layout, and image generation are untouched. Edge function redeploy of ai-generate-ad-variants is required for the server-side copy path to pick this up.

files.latestApprovalRef: 2026-07-18 Dan chat approval ('complete the proposed fix'): regression tests for the parenthetical/banned-word item-name catch-22 — core-name copy validates, full-name copy with 'fresh' inside the item name validates, standalone hype words still fail, and the deterministic fallback for the recon-roast contract shape validates instead of throwing. Prior ref: 2026-07-14 Dan chat approval: add regression coverage for the S10 unmatched-quote coffee-item offer with free cookie and blank optional item values.

files.rationale: Client API wrapper for AI ad generation and poster request payloads. 2026-07-09: readInvokeErrorBody now also reads the numeric wait_seconds from the 429 body and invokeAdEdge threads it through throwInvokeError onto the thrown ErrorWithCode; new getErrorWaitSeconds(e) reads that field (falling back to parsing 'Ns' from the message) so the AI create screen can render a live cooldown countdown. 2026-07-19: BusinessOnboardingContext.access_state includes setup/menu/draft/AI/credit/publish/claim/redeem/billing capability booleans plus limits so UI can read canonical activation-gate state. No request payload, provider, generation, or poster behavior changed.

files.approvalRef: 2026-07-03 initial AI poster core lock; 2026-07-09 Dan chat approval (Approve both): honor merchant-edited headline at publish

files.latestApprovalRef: 2026-07-20 Dan chat approval ('Approve all locked files', OpenAI prepaid-key fallback): the ad-research web-search OpenAI call now routes through the shared _shared/openai-fetch.ts wrapper — OPENAI_API_KEY_PREPAID is tried first and falls back to OPENAI_API_KEY only on HTTP 401/403/404/429 (never on 400/timeout/5xx). Auth-source only: models, prompts, request bodies, provider order, generation caps, timeouts, and cost/telemetry are unchanged; keys are never logged or returned. Validation: npm run gate:ai-poster-lock + npm run typecheck:functions + openai-fetch/research-source-guard tests. Deploy: redeploy ai-generate-ad-variants (and ai-compose-offer). Prior ref: 2026-07-20 Dan chat approval ('deploy my fix now') — F4 image-refusal fix: when the primary image prompt returns no image (evocative branded item names such as 'Sergeant's Stripes' are refused by both providers, previously ~125s then IMAGE_REQUIRED), retry once via Gemini with a category-safe prompt (buildGeminiAdImagePrompt genericizeItems) that drops the brand tokens and asks only for the business-category product, uploading that image as source 'generated'; runs ONLY on the no-image path so successful generations are unchanged, and the exact item identity is still rendered by the app as native text. Prior ref: 2026-07-18 Dan chat approval (ad-copy fallback cost reduction, 'implement the primary fix, and complete the optional follow ups'): generateCopy now records attempt-1 style-gate/diversity rejection reasons (qualityGateRepairFeedback) and appends them to the attempt-2 validationFeedback so the repair prompt names the exact rejected patterns (WEAK_TRY_OUR_PHRASE, POSTER_HEADLINE_REPEATS_LOCKED_OFFER, duplicate-opening, etc.) instead of the generic NO_VALID_VARIANTS message; no generation mechanics, provider routing, offer validation, or poster-layout logic changed. Prior ref: 2026-07-17 Dan chat approval ('I approve items 2 3 and 5'), image-QA calibration so good Gemini images are not needlessly rejected while the OpenAI fallback stays: (item 2) a required item that is present but not prominent is now a warning (ITEM_NOT_PROMINENT), not a discard — only truly absent items, forbidden elements, and crop risk hard-fail a generated image (change lives in lib/quick-deal-image-qa.ts; the generated-image discard gates now key on hardFailReasons/missingItems only); (item 3) the vision inspector is told the real render format — new renderFormat threaded through inspectGeneratedImageForOffer/sourceAwareQaForImageBytes so 4:5 poster images are judged against a vertical 4:5 poster frame instead of the stale square-1:1 rubric (square wording unchanged for 1:1); (item 5) QA outage fails open — an unavailable inspector no longer discards generated/edited/stock images (unavailableDecision returns a VISION_QA_UNAVAILABLE warning for every source type), the discard gates dropped the qa.unavailable clause, and a retry whose inspection is unavailable keeps the first image's known failing verdict (retry_image_qa_unavailable event) so a QA-failed image cannot ship uninspected. Provider order, prompts to the image models, generation caps, cost logging, and the OpenAI fallback are unchanged. Prior ref: 2026-07-17 editable poster text + subheadline revision fix — the poster headline/subheadline are now live-editable fields wired through both preview and publish (same builder inputs, so preview always equals publish); shared POSTER_TEXT_LIMITS (headline 28 / subline 32 / business name 34) are enforced visibly (input maxLength + counters, publish blocks with errPosterTextTooLong/errPosterTextNotAllowed) instead of silently clipping; sanitizePosterCopy records *_TEXT_ADJUSTED warnings whenever sanitizing changed requested text; subheadline/kicker/supporting-line feedback is recognized as copy-only client-side (no unwanted image regeneration or image-credit spend) and enforced server-side (requiresKickerChange intent, kicker included in visible-change fingerprint, kicker-unchanged candidates hard-fail, deterministic fact-safe kicker backstop REVISION_DETERMINISTIC_KICKER); oversized poster candidates are rejected at the gate (POSTER_HEADLINE_OVER_LIMIT / POSTER_KICKER_OVER_LIMIT) and the prompt states the hard poster fit limits and the subheadline->posterKicker revision mapping. Merged 2026-07-18 with the activation-gate capability enforcement (see approvalRef) when the fallback-cost commit f78bd885 was cherry-picked onto the qa branch.
files.approvalRef: 2026-07-07 chat approval: ad_copy reasoningLevel medium->low to fix OPENAI_FETCH_FAILED (gpt-5.4-mini exceeded the ~12s text timeout, dropping the AI image); Part B add AI_IMAGE_MAX_GENERATIONS_PER_REQUEST cap so a missing-item QA verdict never buys a second image (default 2 preserves current behavior); 2026-07-19 Dan chat approval: activation-gate capability enforcement before AI poster/ad generation work
files.rationale: Hosted AI ad generator for copy, image, poster spec, revision, and logging behavior. 2026-07-07: the ad copy call's reasoningLevel changed medium->low so gpt-5.4-mini returns the same validated 5-variant copy in ~10.5s instead of being aborted at ~16s. 2026-07-07 (Part B): a new MAX_IMAGE_GENERATIONS_PER_REQUEST constant (env AI_IMAGE_MAX_GENERATIONS_PER_REQUEST, default 2) gates the QA-triggered image_generation_retry on BOTH the OpenAI and Gemini paths; at 1 a missing-item QA verdict skips the paid regeneration and the first image or existing safe fallback is used, and QA still runs once. 2026-07-19: canonical get_business_capabilities now gates this protected endpoint before quota, credit reservation, logs, provider calls, web search, or image generation; setup-only, lapsed, suspended, or otherwise unentitled businesses fail closed with a 403. No prompt, schema, poster-spec, provider order, generation-cap, or fallback behavior changed. 2026-07-20: the ad-research web-search OpenAI call now authenticates via the shared prepaid/fallback wrapper (OPENAI_API_KEY_PREPAID first, OPENAI_API_KEY on 401/403/404/429); no prompt, model, provider order, cap, timeout, or telemetry change, and neither key is logged or returned.

files.approvalRef: 2026-07-09 Dan chat approval (Approve both): fix coffee-bias — item-congruence rule + category-aware kicker guidance
files.rationale: AI ad-copy prompt, including poster headline and poster kicker guidance. 2026-07-09 (coffee-bias fix, root cause of 'Make it a coffee break' on a 40%-off agujjim deal): the app framing no longer enumerates coffee shops/cafes/bakeries; a VOICE rule requires every customer moment/daypart/occasion to fit the actual item and business category; the posterKicker rule drops the hardcoded coffee-first example list (Coffee break, Lunch pick, Smoothie run, Date night) and instead points at the business's own CATEGORY PLAYBOOK natural customer moments; and a percent-off non-cafe EXAMPLES case teaches the wrong-occasion failure directly. Offer mechanics rules, schema, deal contracts, and all other examples unchanged.
files.latestApprovalRef: 2026-07-18 Dan chat approval (ad-copy fallback cost reduction, 'implement the primary fix, and complete the optional follow ups'): two prompt rules added — VOICE bans starting any output field with 'Try our'/'Try the' (top style-gate rejection in prod telemetry, 26 of 38 rejections in the 10-day window), and poster FIELD RULES ban repeating the locked offer line word-for-word as the poster headline; no schema, fact-safety, or deal-contract changes. Prior ref: 2026-07-17 Dan chat approval ('approved as listed, plus posterTypes.ts'): editable poster text + subheadline revision fix — the poster headline/subheadline are now live-editable fields wired through both preview and publish (same builder inputs, so preview always equals publish); shared POSTER_TEXT_LIMITS (headline 28 / subline 32 / business name 34) are enforced visibly (input maxLength + counters, publish blocks with errPosterTextTooLong/errPosterTextNotAllowed) instead of silently clipping; sanitizePosterCopy records *_TEXT_ADJUSTED warnings whenever sanitizing changed requested text; subheadline/kicker/supporting-line feedback is recognized as copy-only client-side (no unwanted image regeneration or image-credit spend) and enforced server-side (requiresKickerChange intent, kicker included in visible-change fingerprint, kicker-unchanged candidates hard-fail, deterministic fact-safe kicker backstop REVISION_DETERMINISTIC_KICKER); oversized poster candidates are rejected at the gate (POSTER_HEADLINE_OVER_LIMIT / POSTER_KICKER_OVER_LIMIT) and the prompt states the hard poster fit limits and the subheadline->posterKicker revision mapping.

files.approvalRef: 2026-07-03 initial AI poster core lock; 2026-07-09 Dan chat approval (Approve both): coffee-bias regression tests
files.rationale: Regression tests for AI ad prompt rules and poster-format instructions. 2026-07-09: added congruence coverage — a Korean-restaurant percent-off prompt must resolve the restaurant_food playbook, carry the item-congruence rules, and no longer contain the hardcoded coffee-first kicker examples or the coffee-shop-only app framing.
files.latestApprovalRef: 2026-07-18 Dan chat approval (ad-copy fallback cost reduction): regression test added asserting the two new prompt rules (Try-our opening ban, poster locked-offer-line repetition ban) stay in the system prompt. Prior ref: 2026-07-17 Dan chat approval ('approved as listed, plus posterTypes.ts'): editable poster text + subheadline revision fix — the poster headline/subheadline are now live-editable fields wired through both preview and publish (same builder inputs, so preview always equals publish); shared POSTER_TEXT_LIMITS (headline 28 / subline 32 / business name 34) are enforced visibly (input maxLength + counters, publish blocks with errPosterTextTooLong/errPosterTextNotAllowed) instead of silently clipping; sanitizePosterCopy records *_TEXT_ADJUSTED warnings whenever sanitizing changed requested text; subheadline/kicker/supporting-line feedback is recognized as copy-only client-side (no unwanted image regeneration or image-credit spend) and enforced server-side (requiresKickerChange intent, kicker included in visible-change fingerprint, kicker-unchanged candidates hard-fail, deterministic fact-safe kicker backstop REVISION_DETERMINISTIC_KICKER); oversized poster candidates are rejected at the gate (POSTER_HEADLINE_OVER_LIMIT / POSTER_KICKER_OVER_LIMIT) and the prompt states the hard poster fit limits and the subheadline->posterKicker revision mapping.

files.approvalRef: 2026-07-03 initial AI poster core lock

files.approvalRef: 2026-07-13 blanket session approval; Gemini primary image fix
files.latestApprovalRef: 2026-07-20 Dan chat approval ('deploy my fix now') — F4 image-refusal fix: buildGeminiAdImagePrompt gains a genericizeItems option that drops evocative brand tokens (business name, item names, AI concept line) and asks for the business-category product, used as the fallback prompt after a provider content-refusal; all text-free/no-QR/style rules and the branded default path are unchanged. Prior ref: 2026-07-18 Dan chat approval ('do you need to complete item 4 and item 6? ... I approve you to do whatever you need'): item 4 — Gemini 3 Interactions image requests now natively request the output shape via the currently documented response_format field ({type:'image', aspect_ratio, image_size}) so 4:5 poster images are generated at 4:5 instead of relying on prompt prose and client cover-cropping. Safety net for the 2026-07-14 BAD_REQUEST incident class: if the endpoint rejects the request with HTTP 400, the call logs gemini_image_config/response_format_rejected and retries once without response_format, so a Google contract change can never break image generation. Prompts, models, legacy generateContent imageConfig, fallback order, and telemetry shape unchanged. (2026-07-17: a briefly staged no-OpenAI-fallback change was reverted un-deployed at Dan's direction.)
files.rationale: Shared AI image generation provider behavior for poster visuals; Gemini 3 image models use the current Interactions API with native aspect-ratio config and a 400-safe fallback, before OpenAI fallback.

files.approvalRef: 2026-07-13 blanket session approval; Gemini primary image fix
files.latestApprovalRef: 2026-07-20 Dan chat approval ('deploy my fix now') — F4: added a genericizeItems regression test asserting brand tokens (sergeant/colonel/military) are dropped while the business-category product and text-free rules remain. Prior ref: 2026-07-18 Dan chat approval (item 4): the Interactions test now asserts response_format {type:'image', aspect_ratio, image_size} is sent with typed input arrays, and a new regression test proves an HTTP 400 on the response_format request triggers exactly one retry without response_format that succeeds.
files.rationale: Regression tests for shared AI image provider behavior, including Gemini 3 Interactions API routing.

files.approvalRef: 2026-07-20 Dan chat approval ('Approve all locked files', OpenAI prepaid-key fallback): the images/generations and images/edits OpenAI calls now route through the shared _shared/openai-fetch.ts wrapper — OPENAI_API_KEY_PREPAID is tried first and falls back to OPENAI_API_KEY only on HTTP 401/403/404/429 (never on 400/timeout/5xx); the edits multipart body is rebuilt per attempt so the retry sends a fresh form. Auth-source only: image models, prompts, payloads, the gpt-image-1 fallback ladder, IMAGE_CALL_TIMEOUT_MS, and telemetry are unchanged, and neither key is logged or returned. Gemini remains the primary image provider. Validation: npm run gate:ai-poster-lock + npm run typecheck:functions + dalle-image test. Deploy: redeploy ai-generate-ad-variants + ai-compose-offer. Prior ref: 2026-07-07 chat approval: remove gpt-image-2 from the image-model allowlist so the failing primary attempt is skipped (root cause of ai-generate-ad-variants exceeding the 150s edge worker limit)
files.rationale: OpenAI image generation prompt and image handling for poster visuals. 2026-07-07: removed 'gpt-image-2' from OPENAI_IMAGE_MODEL_ALLOWLIST — in prod it fails every request with FETCH_ERROR (hangs to the per-call timeout) before falling back to gpt-image-1, burning the whole image budget and pushing the request past the ~150s worker limit (confirmed via ai_generation_costs: gpt-image-2 ok=false FETCH_ERROR, gpt-image-1 ok=true on every OpenAI image gen). The dashboard OPENAI_IMAGE_MODEL* secret points at gpt-image-2, so dropping it from the allowlist makes pickGenerateModel() fall through to gpt-image-1 directly. IMAGE_CALL_TIMEOUT_MS and all prompts/handling are unchanged from the initial lock. 2026-07-20: the images/generations and images/edits calls authenticate via the shared prepaid/fallback wrapper (OPENAI_API_KEY_PREPAID first, OPENAI_API_KEY on 401/403/404/429; edits body rebuilt per attempt); no model, prompt, payload, timeout, or telemetry change, and neither key is logged or returned.

files.approvalRef: 2026-07-07 chat approval: update the model-selection regression test to assert gpt-image-2 is rejected and falls back to gpt-image-1
files.rationale: Regression tests for OpenAI image generation prompt behavior. 2026-07-07: the model-secret test now asserts a gpt-image-2 generate-model secret falls back to gpt-image-1 (gpt-image-2 removed from the allowlist because it fails every prod request).
